title: Number Type in Formula Column
excerpt: Change the number “type” in formula column (ex: $, %)

posts.author: kaylak (Kayla K)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @jmohammedsfpbs ,

If you click the summary at the bottom of the formula column, the customization options will appear. You can select the pre existing units or add your own.

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml: I have been looking to add currency to Formula Columns without them no longer being numbers for soo long! THANK. YOU.

posts.author: jmohammedsfpbs (Jada Mohammed Acuna)
posts.cookedHtml: omg! Thanks! This is going to change my life lol

posts.author: Flaviomian (Flavio Mian)
posts.cookedHtml: That would be swell… Also another good change could be the ability to round numbers by desired decimals.

posts.author: ehjay (Ehjay)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks. Just because of your question I signup to say thank you very much.

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml: Just for everyone’s information: unfortunately the summary-line below the column does not exist for subitems. This would be nice to be addressed by the devs.
